title: CEO Assistant (Execution & External Coordination Focus)
description:
🧩 Job Responsibilities

1)     Act as a core support to the CEO, assisting with daily work matters and business-related follow-ups;

2)     Liaise and coordinate with financial institutions and third-party professional service providers (including banks, brokers, custodians, and other service firms) to support the execution of related matters;

3)     Attend and follow up on all key meetings of the CEO, including meeting preparation, note-taking, action-item tracking, and execution follow-ups;

4)     Assist the CEO in coordinating with partners related to each asset portfolio, and follow up on collaboration progress and related matters;

5)     Support cross-team and cross-institution communication and coordination within the scope authorized by the CEO;

6)     Handle ad hoc and special tasks assigned by the CEO.

👤 Job Requirements

✅ Mandatory Requirements

1)     Strong sense of responsibility: Highly responsible, detail-oriented, and accountable for work outcomes;

2)     Execution & efficiency: Strong execution capability and efficiency, able to deliver high-quality results within set timelines;

3)     Flexibility: Able to provide necessary support outside regular working hours when required;

4)     Professional ethics: High level of professional integrity, capable of handling sensitive company and entity-related information appropriately;

5)     High time flexibility: Able to adapt to non-fixed working hours.

Note: This position is not suitable for candidates seeking fixed working hours.

⭐ Preferred Qualifications

1)     Relevant experience: More than 3 years of experience in liaising with financial institutions, investment, asset management, or related third-party service providers; prior experience as a CEO / Founder / Senior Executive Assistant is a strong plus;

2)     Relevant knowledge: Background or exposure to finance, accounting, or legal-related fields is a plus.

3)     Language skills: Strong English communication skills, able to participate in English meetings, especially those involving U.S. time zones;

4)     Work methodology: Logical and structured working style, with strong task decomposition and execution capability;

5)     Communication skills: Strong communication skills, able to efficiently relay information between the CEO and multiple stakeholders;

6)     Learning & problem-solving ability: Strong ability to work independently and resolve issues; work capability and efficiency are core evaluation criteria for this role.

💼 Compensation & Benefits

  • Monthly salary: SGD 7,000 – 10,000, subject to experience and overall capability;
  • Compensation and incentive structure will be discussed further during the interview process;
  • Performance-based bonus ranging from 3 to 8 months’ salary, subject to individual and company performance.

📌 Additional Information

  • This position is limited to 1 hire, selection will be based on merit;
  • Working hours are relatively flexible and require alignment with U.S. time zone meetings;
  • This role is best suited for candidates with strong stress tolerance, high execution capability, and a desire to work closely with core business operations;
  • This is a high-trust role with clear long-term development opportunities;
